Bonus: Jeff Walker's Answers to Posts/Questions
Q&A
Q: I have a question about the Headline and ethical bribe. These two critical pieces are for something of value that you are giving to your prospective customer for free to get them to Opt-In, correct. The headline and ethical bribe are not about the "seed launch--paid for" offering right?
A: The headline and the ethical bribe are to get people to join your list. After they join the list, then eventually you will run your launch sequence for your Seed Launch. I covered that sequence in Module/Call #4.
So the answer is YES, the bribe is a value piece that people get for opting in to your list.
Q: Is the headline supposed to be broad and describe what you would get if you buy the entire course/program/product or is it supposed to get you excited enough about the ethical bribe that you opt-in and get on my list and I can do a full launch to sell you my product/program/course/broad initiative?
A: The ONLY job of the headline is to get them to join your list. So it's super focused on the ethical bribe... what you're going to give them for joining your list. Don't worry about your eventual program... selling that is what your Seed Launch is all about.
Think of it this way... if a guy is interested in a girl, when he asks her for a date, should he be telling her about the wedding and the family he has planned for his eventual wife? In most cases, that wouldn't work so well, would it? Instead, he asks her out to dinner... builds a relationship, and then worries about the big future questions.
Q: A question for folks who have used and tested opt-in forms before: what effect does including or omitting the name field from an opt-in form have, on the number of opt-ins received?
A: You have to think about what having that name or not having it would mean to your business strategically. I started testing this in 1999, and I found that eliminating the first name raised opt-ins by more than 10% (that was in the stock market niche). Further tests since then have confirmed similar results in other niches.
HOWEVER, there are times where it would be worth losing that 10%.
In my current market (teaching people how to build their businesses), many people will put in a fake name... so using that first name in an email ends up being very counterproductive.
Q: Are we supposed to create a high-ticket product?
Don't get me wrong, I'm not opposed to that  however, I am not sure how I can create a $497-997 product for Stress Management.
Anyone have some insight in this?
A: Just because I sold my Seed Launch product for $997 doesn't mean that's the price range everyone should be charging. It's completely niche-dependent. If you're selling something on crochet, it's not going to be $997.
Q: Is there a difference between the marketing pages you can get with lead pages.net vs. the ones you can get in Kajabi?
A: Kajabi can do a very good job... and if you already have it, you should probably stick with it.
We've been using Kajabi for several years, but we're now using Leadpages.net... it's more flexible and has more designs. And for people who don't have Kajabi, it's a fraction of the price.
Q: I was looking for info on leadpages.net website but couldn't tell ...Does it do everything Kajabi does? Like membership sites for when I set up an online program?
A: No, it doesn't. Leadpages is a more focused on one thing - it creates squeeze pages, sales pages, video pages, etc.. It's a LOT simpler to use than Kajabi for those things, and it's also a lot more flexible.
HOWEVER, It doesn't create video courses like Kajabi... and they don't have full launch funnels yet (although they will soon.)
I shot a video about building squeeze pages with Leadpages, and I also have comparisons vs Kajabi. We'll be releasing that video today here on this page.
Q: In order to creating headlines and Ethical Bribes, should we already have niche we want to target ? I am a complete newbie in trying to learn this. Thanks for any and all help.
A: If you don't have your niche nailed down, then pick one that you're thinking about and do the homework around that one
Q: On 24 of the 50 headlines..my brain is starting to work overtime..
A: Just put your fingers on the keyboard and start typing. The time for editing and analysis is later. They don't need to be good, they just need to be done. Once they're done, you will have some gold buried somewhere in the 50
Q: Will you provide a timeline for the seed launch? In PLF, you do give a timeline for it, but I mean a timeline for starting from scratch (all the pre-work), including pre-prelunch, "shot across the bow", all the sideways sales letter stuff, pre-launch content, etc. -- not just the delivery and surveys.
I've logged into PLF again to review and just feel overwhelmed with it all. Maybe I'm missing something, but I'd like a "Day 1, Day 2, etc." timeline which starts with the pre-work like the "shot across the bow" (or whatever is first).
A: I will absolutely give you a timeline.
AND THIS PART IS IMPORTANT: I hadn't necessarily planned on doing so... but you make a great case for it, and a huge part of the Seed Launch is listening to your students. I'm delivering this course as a Seed Launch, so I'm all about listening to requests. You've done me a great service in asking for this and letting me know why it would be valuable.
That doesn't mean that I'll fulfill every request - and you shouldn't either when you deliver your Seed Launch. There is no end to what people will request, and if you fill everyone of them you'll exhaust yourself, and you'll end up with a bloated product. But you certainly want to listen to the requests, and when you hear a great one, then you act on it.
Finally, to give a little information on this topic - timelines will always vary. ESPECIALLY with Seed Launches. So nothing will ever be locked in stone (and, of course, you already knew that)... but I'll give you guidelines and the logic behind the thinking.
Q: (continuation of the above Q) It will be really helpful to have an A-Z, from a "shot across the bow" to "sales!" timeline. Should it be 14, 30, 60 days, etc.
A: 14... or less.
I think my total timeline last week was maybe 7 days from "shot across the bow" all the way to cart close. Some might be a little longer - but this isn't a drawn out process.
Q: What is an example of an "ethical bribe" to get people to sign up? is it a "cheat sheet" giveaway or video, or is it more like "sign up and be entered to win a prize"?
A: You're right on with your first examples. It would typically be a cheat sheet, whitepaper, training video (or series of). It's not typically an enter to win a prize - primarily (to my thinking) because a big part of the value of a bribe is that you get to start building relationship with those who opt in - which lends itself to you starting to add value via some sort of teaching - as opposed to doing a random giveaway. I'm sure there are times to use a giveaway as a bribe - but building a seed list probably isn't the time. Ideally, you want to give away something that has high perceived value and something that has continuity with the offer you are eventually going to pitch.
Q: The Mini Squeeze pages (the ones with a full image in the background and the small opt-in box).
Okay, how do you make those? Is this a plugin?
A: As I mentioned on the call... my team and I are using Leadpages.net. It's super easy to use.
Q: Do I really need to invest in a third-party landing/squeeze page company if I'm already familiar with building websites? I guess what I'm asking is what will I be missing if I didn't use a third party site to assist with my squeeze page? Is there some sort of back-end functionality that I need outside of the page capturing names, emails, and placing them into the proper list?
A: No... if you've got the skills to build web sites, you won't be missing anything (other than having tested designs to work from... and you've already seen a bunch of tested designs in the webinar.) There isn't any backend functionality other than adding people to the email list.
Q: Do we have to prepare anything for the call? like is there anything we should have or do before our meeting? I have a feeling that this training is a big deal and I really want to show up prepared and fully present. what do you think?
A: Most important thing is to show up with an open mind and an open heart and be ready to receive.
Next, be thinking about what you could offer people to get them to join your email list... we think of this as an "ethical bribe". What piece of knowledge of wisdom can you offer to people who subscribe to your email list?
Q: I have no product, no list and I am so scared to start this.
A: Please don't be scared... there's only two outcomes possible. You either are successful, or you get an incredible education. Neither of those outcomes are anything to be scared of...
